BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Back-of-napkin before the annual planning review with David Kim. Testing whether the practice covers fixed costs if a quarter runs light on keynotes.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Revisit with David Kim once 2025 actuals close and S-corp reasonable-compensation split is set.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is roughly 4 billed consulting days per month at current cost structure. With four anchor engagements plus coaching, there is ample headroom even in a light keynote quarter.
ActionItems: Confirm reasonable-compensation split with David Kim before Q1 estimated taxes
Revisit after year-end with proper modeling and the pending Ellison and ALD proposals
